title: dryopteris thelypteris subsp. squamigera
intro: Dryopteris thelypteris subsp. squamigera, also known as the Japanese wood fern, is a subspecies of the Dryopteris thelypteris plant, characterized by its unique, scaly rhizome. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its use in traditional medicine. The Japanese wood fern has been used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. The plant can be prepared as herbal tea or infused oil, and is also used in traditional Japanese medicine, Kampo, to support overall health and well-being.
